meaning: vt.执行,实行,完成,处死,制成,[律]经签名盖章等手续使(证书)生效
examples: We are going to execute our campaign plan to the letter.
We execute that instruction and we move to the next one.
The lawyer argued that it is wrong to execute a person for a crime he committed while he was a minor.
If Cao is correct and is able to execute his idea, Internet addresses could be toast by 2010.
He believes it is important to our national security interests to execute on that policy.
